header.title: Forms
header.subtitle: How forms are built in this project: React Hook Form for state management, Zod factories for i18n-aware validation, and UI primitives wired directly via register. Three real forms from the codebase, made interactive below.

gallery.title: Form gallery
gallery.description: Interactive replicas of the real forms. Validation is active (Zod). Submit is a no-op demo — no side effects, no real login, no data saved.

login.title: Login form
login.description: Auth form with email + password, server error block, and inline validation. Errors go inline (not as toasts) because the toast would disappear before the user can re-read and retry.
login.note: Why inline errors for login: a toast dismisses in ~4 s. If the user types a wrong password and gets a toast, it vanishes before they can correct it. The inline error block persists.

settings.title: Settings form
settings.description: Profile form with two-column layout, bio textarea, optional URL field, and isDirty guard. Save is disabled until the user changes something.
settings.note: Why isDirty guard: prevents unnecessary API calls when nothing has changed. The button label switches to 'Saving…' during submission.

user.title: User form
user.description: Entity creation form with name, email, role, and status selects. Typical pattern for domain entities with enum fields.
user.note: Domain forms (user, report…) live in features/*/components/ and are consumed via the feature barrel. The schema lives in lib/validators/ (deuda técnica: should be in features/users/schemas/).

validation.nameMin: Name must be at least 2 characters

schema.title: Schema with i18n factory
schema.description: All schemas export a createXxxSchema(messages) factory. Messages are injected by the component after calling useTranslations — so the same schema works in any locale without re-importing.

timing.title: Validation timing
timing.description: Default: onSubmit. The first submission triggers all validators; subsequent changes re-validate onChange. Change to onBlur for a stricter UX or onChange for immediate feedback.

errors.title: Server errors: inline vs toast
errors.description: Validation errors always go inline via the error prop. Server errors go to toast.error — except on the login screen where errors must persist until the user retries.

state.title: Form state: isSubmitting, isDirty, isValid
state.description: Use isSubmitting for the Button loading prop (spinner built in). Use isDirty to guard Save buttons on edit forms. Avoid isValid as a sole guard — it can show errors before the user has typed anything.

async.title: Async submit: mutateAsync vs mutate
async.description: Use mutateAsync + try/catch when you need to close a dialog only on success. Use mutate with onSuccess/onError callbacks for fire-and-forget patterns. Toast always lives in the component, never in the hook.

field.title: Field-level errors
field.description: Pass errors.fieldName?.message to the error prop of the wrapper component. The wrappers (Input, Textarea, Select…) render the error text below the field in the error semantic color.

ai.title: Working with AI
ai.description: If you work with Claude, Cursor, or another AI tool on this template, form conventions live in .claude/rules/forms.md. That rule is loaded automatically when the AI touches files in src/components/forms/** or src/lib/validators/**. This page teaches the human; the rules file instructs the AI. We keep both in sync.
